privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the standard ImageJ FluorescentCells.tif sample image, which is real data. The setup_task.sh script tries to download the image directly as a .tif, which results in a 404 (the official file is packaged as a .zip), creating an empty file. Analysis of the true FluorescentCells.tif confirms it has 3 channels with dimensions of 512x512. However, the claim that the image is a Z-stack with 51 slices is completely contradicted; the official ImageJ sample is a 2D composite image with only 1 Z-slice. The task requires creating output files at specific paths (standard_mip.png, depth_coded_projection.png, stack_report.txt). The verifier validates the depth color coding by checking an HSV histogram for at least 3 significant bins, meaning the min_color_variance claim of 0.2 is unused and unverified.
